name: I-Ching System
coreNature: Ancient Chinese divination and philosophical system encoding the fundamental patterns of change through binary combinatorics
description: The I-Ching represents the most sophisticated ancient system for mapping the fundamental patterns of change and transformation. Through its 64 hexagrams formed from eight trigram combinations, it provides a complete archetypal language for understanding the dynamics of any situation. As one of the three primary systems integrated within the MahaMaya framework (alongside DNA and Tarot), the I-Ching contributes the temporal-transformational dimension, offering both practical divination methodology and profound philosophical insights into the nature of change itself. Its binary structure parallels both genetic code organization and computational logic, making it a crucial bridge between ancient wisdom and modern understanding.
essence: Eight trigrams generating 64 hexagrams (2^3 × 2^3 = 2^6) as cosmic alphabet of change
structure: Eight fundamental trigrams (Bagua) combining to form 64 hexagrams, each with six line positions representing hierarchical levels of manifestation from earth to heaven

name: Qian
description: Qian embodies pure, unadulterated Yang energy – the primal power that initiates all creation. As Heaven, it represents the cosmic father principle, unlimited potential, and the ceaseless movement of celestial forces.

name: Qian
description: Pure unadulterated Yang energy - the primal power that initiates all creation. Six unbroken lines represent strength, dynamic action, persistence, divine influence, leadership, and beginnings. Success through unwavering perseverance aligned with high principles and timing.

name: Tai
description: Harmony, prosperity, and successful flow. Heaven (creative Yang) below tends upward while Earth (receptive Yin) above tends downward. Natural interaction brings balance and flourishing. High and low connect, leading to understanding and mutual benefit.

name: Da Zhuang
description: Great power, strength, and vigorous advance. Thunder energized by Heaven below indicates peak Yang energy. Power must be used correctly with justice and propriety. Reckless force leads to trouble. Strength tempered with awareness.

name: Xiao Chu
description: Gentle restraint yielding small, gradual successes. Wind blows over powerful Heaven, representing subtle force holding back stronger one. Like dense clouds gathering potential but not yet bringing rain. Progress bit by bit through patience and minor adjustments.

name: Xu
description: Necessary waiting or pause. Strength and potential (Heaven) present but facing obstacle or danger ahead (Water), like clouds gathering before rain. Rushing forward would be perilous. Focus on nourishment - both literal and metaphorical - while waiting with patience and sincerity.

name: Da You
description: Abundance, wealth, and success. Fire (clarity, brilliance) high above Heaven (strength, substance), like sun illuminating everything. Peak achievement and influence. Importance of humility and fairness in managing abundance ensures continued fortune.

name: Da Chu
description: Restraining great power, accumulating large resources. Mountain above Heaven indicates containment and cultivation of powerful energy. Not just holding back but actively cultivating strength, character, and knowledge. Building reserves for right time.

name: Guai
description: Breakthrough, determination, and decisive resolution. Lake above Heaven suggests overflow requiring release. Time for resolute action against negative elements. Must combine strength with caution, avoiding violence while being firm.

name: Kun
description: Kun embodies pure, undiluted Yin energy – the receptive, nurturing power that complements the Creative. As Earth, it represents the cosmic mother principle, infinite capacity, and the power to manifest and sustain all forms of life.

name: Pi
description: Stagnation, obstruction, and lack of communication. Heaven above tends upward, Earth below tends downward - forces move apart. Communication blocked, energy doesn't flow. Time of discord where inferior elements gain influence. Wise person withdraws and preserves integrity.

name: Kun
description: Pure undiluted Yin energy - the receptive, nurturing power that complements the Creative. Signifies devotion, responsiveness, capacity, motherhood, the earth itself, and the potential to bring things into form and sustain them. Success through adapting, supporting, and following correct initiative.

name: Yu
description: Enthusiasm, movement, and mobilizing energy. Thunder bursts forth from receptive Earth. Following humility, represents time for inspiring action and celebration. Single Yang line leads receptive Yin lines. Foresight needed to ensure enthusiasm is well-directed.

name: Guan
description: Contemplation, viewing from higher perspective, observing patterns. Wind (gentle influence) moves over Earth (receptive masses), suggesting vantage point for understanding. Looking and being looked at. Understanding through detached observation.

name: Bi
description: Union, alliance, and seeking support. Water (flowing, adapting) flows over Earth (receptive, supportive), like water naturally finding its place on land. Union should arise organically around a worthy central figure. Sincerity and mutual support vital.

name: Jin
description: Progress, promotion, and rapid advance like sun rising over earth. Fire ascends above receptive Earth indicating growing influence and recognition. Progress is smooth and unopposed but should be grounded in virtue to be sustainable.

name: Bo
description: Disintegration, erosion, and stripping away. Five Yin lines undermining single Yang line at top - Mountain resting precariously on eroding Earth. Time when inferior forces ascendant. Advice is to adapt, protect essential, and wait for cycle to turn.

name: Cui
description: Gathering, massing, and collection. Lake above Earth suggests waters collecting in one place. Time for assembly requiring central figure and shared purpose. Preparation needed as gathering creates both opportunity and danger.

name: Zhen
description: Zhen represents the sudden arousal of energy, like thunder breaking the silence or the first shoots of spring bursting through frozen ground. It embodies initiative, shock that awakens, and the power of new beginnings.

name: Wu Wang
description: Innocence, naturalness, acting without ulterior motives. Heaven above Thunder suggests action springing naturally from inherent correctness. Acting spontaneously according to time without calculating gain. Warns that incorrect action brings misfortune.

name: Fu
description: Turning point after extreme decline, return of light and positive energy. Thunder stirs beneath receptive Earth, marking new cycle beginning like Winter Solstice. Natural spontaneous return to goodness. Recognize subtle beginning and nurture gently.

name: Zhen
description: Shock, arousal, and awakening. Thunder doubled brings repeated shock that awakens and terrifies initially but leads to relief and joy. Fear brings introspection and reverence. After shock comes laughter of relief. Maintaining composure during upheaval.

name: Yi
description: Increase, benefit, and expansion. Wind above Thunder suggests spreading beneficial influence. Time of growth where sacrifice of higher benefits lower. Favorable for undertakings and movement. Using good fortune to help others.

name: Zhun
description: The difficult, chaotic situation at the beginning of a new undertaking. Like a tender sprout (Thunder) trying to push through resistant soil while navigating a storm (Water). Inherent potential for growth faces immediate obstacles requiring patience and seeking capable helpers.

name: Shi Ke
description: Forcefully removing obstruction, like biting through something stuck between teeth. Need for decisive action in administering justice. Clarity (Fire) combines with energetic movement (Thunder) to cut through difficulties. Requires firmness and fairness.

name: Yi
description: Nourishment both physical and spiritual. Image of open mouth with firm jaws above and below. Mountain stillness above Thunder movement below suggests balance of rest and action. Mindfulness of consumption and production of words and actions.

name: Sui
description: Following, adapting, and moving in response to leadership or changing conditions. Thunder (arousing movement) below initiates action while Lake (joy, openness) above responds pleasurably. Suggests willingly following worthy cause or adapting joyfully to flow of events.

name: Xun
description: Xun embodies gentle but persistent influence, like wind shaping landscapes over time or roots penetrating soil. It represents subtle power, adaptability, and the ability to influence through persistence rather than force.

name: Gou
description: Unexpected encounter, temptation, and subtle infiltration. Wind beneath Heaven suggests hidden influence creeping in. Single Yin line at bottom threatens to undermine Yang above. Caution against seductive or corrupting influences.

name: Sheng
description: Ascending growth and effort pushing upward. Wood beneath Earth suggests plant pushing through soil. Gradual but persistent advance through effort. Success through steady determination and seeking guidance from authority.

name: Heng
description: Permanence, continuity, and long-lasting relationships. Thunder above Wind signifies dynamic action grounded in steady influence. Established patterns and enduring principles. Success from maintaining constancy in path and character.

name: Xun
description: Gentle penetration and subtle influence. Wind doubled shows continuous gentle pressure that penetrates everywhere. Success through gentle persistence rather than force. Small effects accumulate into significant change. Following orders, adapting.

name: Jing
description: The well as source of nourishment and social organization. Water beneath Wood suggests drawing water up. Well remains in place serving all. Represents inexhaustible source when properly maintained. Failure if rope too short or pitcher broken.

name: Ding
description: The sacrificial vessel, nourishment, and cultural refinement. Fire over Wood suggests cooking and transformation. Sacred vessel for offering to heaven and nourishing people. Represents civilization, culture, and spiritual nourishment through transformation.

name: Gu
description: Decay, stagnation, and need to repair damage from neglect or complacency. Wind below creates conditions for rot, arrested by Mountain stillness above. Signifies correcting errors, often inherited from past. Requires decisive action and responsibility.

name: Da Guo
description: Extraordinary situation where strong elements excessive, overpowering weaker elements. Like beam thick in middle but weak at ends, on verge of collapse. Pressure, stress, imbalance requiring exceptional measures. Critical situation needing careful handling.

name: Kan
description: Kan represents water in its dangerous aspect - the abyss, the gorge, the depths. Yet within danger lies sincerity and the potential for wisdom. It embodies both peril and the flow that finds its way through any obstacle.

name: Song
description: Contention, disputes, and litigation. The outward-striving nature (Heaven) clashes with the inwardly directed nature (Water). Fundamental opposition of forces. Strongly advises caution: avoid initiating conflict, seek impartial arbitration, find middle ground.

name: Shi
description: Disciplined organization and collective action. Water (danger, hidden potential, masses) contained within Earth (receptivity, obedience). Success requires strong, experienced leadership trusted by the masses. Discipline, clear purpose, and careful strategy essential.

name: Xie
description: Release from tension, liberation, and relief. Thunder emerges from Water like spring rains dissolving ice. Clearing away obstacles following obstruction. Time for prompt action to consolidate release, forgive wrongs, let go of burdens.

name: Huan
description: Dissolution, dispersion, and breaking up rigidity. Wind over Water suggests wind driving over water, dispersing it. Breaking up selfish hardness through gentleness. Religious gatherings dissolve egotism. Success through overcoming separateness.

name: Kan
description: Danger doubled, immersion in difficulties. Water repeated signifies compounded risk. Points to learning through practice and maintaining sincerity amidst peril. Like water flowing despite obstacles, must adapt and persist to navigate danger successfully.

name: Wei Ji
description: Before completion, on verge of success, transition incomplete. Fire over Water - forces not yet in harmony. Young fox almost across stream but wets tail. Caution needed at critical moment. Spring about to break forth. Hope and potential.

name: Meng
description: Ignorance and inexperience with potential for learning, like a spring (Water) emerging at the foot of a mountain (Mountain). The mountain blocks clear view while water below is obscure. Describes the student-teacher relationship requiring guidance, patience, and sincerity.

name: Kun
description: Oppression, exhaustion, and being hemmed in. Lake above Water suggests water drained away, leaving emptiness. Time of hardship where resources depleted. Success through inner strength, maintaining cheerfulness despite adversity.

name: Li
description: Li represents fire that clings to its fuel, consciousness that depends on objects of awareness. It embodies clarity, intelligence, and beauty, but also the need for something to illuminate and attach to.

name: Tong Ren
description: Community, partnership, and gathering people under clear principles. Fire illuminates while Heaven provides strength and direction. Success from shared goals, public transparency, and overcoming selfish biases. Universal fellowship rather than exclusive cliques.

name: Ming Yi
description: Injury or concealment of brightness, intelligence suppressed. Fire beneath Earth signifies wisdom forced underground due to hostile circumstances. Dangerous to display talents openly. Conceal light, endure adversity, maintain inner clarity while adapting outwardly.

name: Feng
description: Peak abundance and fullness like sun at zenith. Thunder and Lightning (Fire) together bring maximum brilliance and movement. Time of greatness but must be used wisely as peak cannot last. Be like sun at midday - shine on all impartially.

name: Jia Ren
description: Structure and proper functioning within family or close group. Wind arises from Fire suggesting effective conduct stems from inner clarity. Emphasis on distinct roles, loyalty, consistency. Woman's role often highlighted as central to family well-being.

name: Ji Ji
description: Completion, perfect order, equilibrium achieved. Water over Fire - all lines in proper places. Perfect balance but contains seeds of disorder. Transition from order to disorder natural. Small matters still succeed. Vigilance needed to maintain.

name: Li
description: Brightness, clarity, and consciousness. Fire repeated signifies intense illumination but also dependence, like fire needing fuel. Highlights intellectual clarity and visibility. Success from recognizing dependence and cultivating inner docility.

name: Bi
description: Beauty, grace, and attention to form. Fire at foot of Mountain illuminates and beautifies its base. Importance of aesthetics and presentation, but warns against mistaking superficial form for true substance. Grace appropriate for minor matters.

name: Ge
description: Revolution, transformation, and radical change. Fire within Lake suggests conflicting forces requiring transformation. Like animal molting old skin. Change must be carefully timed and based on necessity. After completion, new order must be established.

name: Gen
description: Gen embodies the principle of stillness and stopping, like a mountain that marks boundaries and provides stability. It represents meditation, contemplation, and knowing when and where to stop.

name: Dun
description: Timely strategic withdrawal to preserve strength. Heaven moving away from Mountain, pulling back from entanglement with inferior forces. Voluntary retreat to avoid damage and maintain integrity. Keeping distance from negativity allows remaining unharmed.

name: Qian
description: Humility and achieving balance by bringing down the high and elevating the low. Mountain (normally high) places itself beneath Earth (normally lower). Voluntary self-decrease creates equilibrium. Modesty as source of strength and sustainability.

name: Xiao Guo
description: Small exceeding, minor excess, exceptional modesty. Thunder over Mountain - movement restricted by obstacles. Bird flying - must come down. Success in small matters through exceptional conscientiousness. Not time for great undertakings.

name: Jian
description: Gradual, steady development like wild goose migration. Wood on Mountain suggests tree growing slowly on mountain. Progress through proper stages cannot be rushed. Marriage as example of gradual development through proper ceremonies.

name: Jian
description: Obstruction and difficulty in movement, like walking with limp. Danger lies ahead while stillness is behind. Progress blocked. Wise course is recognizing impediment, stopping, seeking allies, finding alternatives. Perseverance through withdrawal.

name: Lu
description: Travel, being stranger, and transience. Fire on Mountain burns briefly then moves on. Wanderer has no permanent home, must be cautious and adaptable. Success through remaining reserved, just, and not imposing. Avoiding conflicts as outsider.

name: Gen
description: Stillness, meditation, and rest. Mountain doubled emphasizes complete stillness and stopping. Rest when it is time to rest, act when time to act. Inner tranquility through keeping still. Knowing when to stop prevents going too far.

name: Xian
description: Mutual attraction, influence, and courtship between opposites. Receptive Lake above firm Mountain signifies natural attraction and responsiveness. Mountain stillness provides foundation for Lake joyful response. Beginning phase of attraction.

name: Dui
description: Dui represents joy and openness, like a lake that reflects the sky and invites gathering. It embodies communication, pleasure, and the attractive power of genuine happiness, though it warns against superficial pleasure.

name: Lu
description: Correct conduct and navigating delicate situations. Heaven (strength, authority) above Lake (joy, openness). Like treading on a tiger's tail - need for caution, awareness, and adherence to proper forms. Success through careful steps and respecting hierarchy.

name: Lin
description: Approach, arrival, and beneficial influence of superior presence drawing near. Earth (receptive, nurturing) above Lake (joyful, open) depicts benevolent authority nearing people. Auspicious time of growth but temporary - carries warning of future decline.

name: Gui Mei
description: Subordinate position, compromise, and accepting secondary role. Thunder over Lake suggests impulsive attraction leading to unequal relationship. Young girl marrying as concubine. Undertakings bring misfortune due to improper position.

name: Zhong Fu
description: Inner truth, sincerity, and confidence. Wind over Lake suggests gentle influence on joyous receptivity. Empty center like bird's egg - gentle and devoted. Truth that influences even difficult beings. Success through genuine understanding and empathy.

name: Jie
description: Limitation, moderation, and creating boundaries. Water over Lake suggests limited amount of water - necessity of measure. Fixed limits make world comprehensible. But galling limitations should not be persevered in. Balance between necessary structure and excessive restriction.

name: Kui
description: Fundamental opposition and divergence. Fire rising upward above Lake seeping downward - natures move opposite directions. Conflict from differing goals. While major union difficult, small matters might find temporary agreement. Highlights challenge of bridging gaps.

name: Sun
description: Decrease, reduction, and sacrifice for higher purpose. Mountain above Lake suggests diminishing lower to benefit higher. Not loss but redistribution - decreasing excess to augment what is lacking. Sincerity in sacrifice brings benefit.

name: Dui
description: Joy, pleasure, and open communication. Lake doubled emphasizes infectious joy and mutual encouragement. True joy comes from inner steadfastness, not external pleasures. Success through creating atmosphere of joy that encourages others.

name: Genetic Code Architecture
coreNature: The foundational layer containing nucleotides, base pairs, and all 64 DNA codons that encode biological information
description: The complete genetic architecture from individual nucleotides through base pairs to the full 64-codon system, representing the fundamental language through which life writes itself into existence.

name: Adenine
coreNature: The purine nucleotide representing expansive, outward-moving energy in the genetic code
description: Adenine stands as the primary yang nucleotide, embodying the expansive, outward-reaching force in genetic architecture. As a purine with its double-ring structure, it carries greater informational complexity. Its partnership with Thymine through double hydrogen bonds creates the foundational stability of the DNA double helix while maintaining the flexibility necessary for replication and transcription.
essence: The expanding force in genetic architecture, representing active, solar principles

coreNature: The double adenine pair representing maximum expansive force, pure yang energy in dyadic form
description: The AA pair represents the doubling of expansive force, creating regions of intense outward pressure in the genetic code. Like the sun at midday, it radiates pure active energy without restraint.
essence: Doubled expansion creating intense outward-moving force

name: Codon_AAA

description: AAA represents the pure yang force taken to its trinitarian extreme, encoding lysine, the positively charged amino acid that reaches out to form connections.

As a pyrimidine with single-ring structure, it provides the receptive simplicity that balances Adenine's complex assertion.
